summary: A suicide attack on a Manbij restaurant killed four Americans and over a dozen others.
The SDF declared the territorial defeat of ISIL after driving fighters from Baghouz.
Damascus opened a new ground and air campaign against opposition-held parts of northwestern Syria.
Erdogan said Damascus was attempting to wreck Turkish-Russian cooperation through its Idlib campaign.
Moscow said Syrian troops had begun a unilateral ceasefire in Idlib at midnight.
Ankara and Washington reached a deal on co-managing a border buffer zone in Syria.
A Moscow-brokered truce halted regime aerial bombardment of the rebel-held northwest after months of strikes.
US Central Command said it had struck an al-Qaeda-linked group near Idlib.
The White House announced US troops would step back as Turkey readied a cross-border operation.
Turkey began its offensive against Kurdish-led forces in northeast Syria.
A US-brokered pause gave Kurdish fighters one-hundred-twenty hours to leave a planned border safe zone.
Erdogan travelled to Sochi to meet Putin as the US-brokered pause neared expiry.
A Russia-Turkey deal required Kurdish fighters to leave a border strip within one-hundred-fifty hours.
US forces raided a compound near Barisha and killed ISIL chief Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi.
US officials said two adult men had been captured during the Idlib raid.
ISIL acknowledged Baghdadi's death and identified Qurayshi (Abu Ibrahim al-Hashimi) as the new caliph.
Ankara urged Moscow to broker a fresh truce as bombing intensified across rebel-held areas.
Turkey said six of its soldiers had died in artillery strikes blamed on Damascus.
Putin and Erdogan announced a fresh Idlib truce after marathon talks in Moscow.
Hay'et Tahrir al-Sham said it would not respect the new Russia-Turkey arrangement.
A bombing at a Manbij restaurant killed four Americans, including service members and a contractor.
The Pentagon identified by name three of those killed in the Manbij bombing.
US-backed forces announced the end of ISIS territorial control after seizing Baghouz.
Turkish troops began crossing into northeast Syria as Erdogan declared the operation under way.
A US position near Kobani came under Turkish shellfire; displaced numbers passed seventy thousand.
Turkish officials claimed Ras al-Ayn had fallen, though Kurdish forces and monitors disputed that.
Trump tweeted that Syria and Assad should be the ones protecting Kurds against Ankara.
The US Treasury moved to sanction Turkish ministries and officials over the cross-border offensive.
A US-brokered deal paused fighting for five days to let Kurdish forces pull back.
After Americans left their main Manbij base, Russian and Syrian government forces moved in.
Erdogan met Putin in Sochi to negotiate the next phase of the planned safe zone.
Trump said the truce was now permanent and announced he was lifting Turkey sanctions.
US commandos raided an Idlib compound and killed Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi, the ISIS chief.
NPR reported that helicopter fire during the Baghdadi raid had killed and maimed Syrian farmers.
The Senate passed the Caesar Act sanctions on Syria as part of annual defense legislation.
A Russia-backed Idlib push had uprooted more than forty thousand people in four days.
Six Turkish military personnel died in Idlib shelling that Ankara attributed to Syrian forces.
The UN humanitarian chief said nearly one million people had fled Idlib since December.
After Moscow talks, Putin and Erdogan unveiled a fresh Idlib ceasefire starting at midnight.
A defector who photographed regime torture victims, known by the alias Caesar, testified to senators.
US Central Command opened a credibility assessment into civilian casualty claims from the Baghdadi raid.
The Pentagon rejected reports that civilians had died in the Baghdadi operation, citing hostile intent.
